Analysis: Likely caused by import of torch_geometric/ogb (version conflict), multiprocessing in DataLoader (num_workers=2), large torch.load of 350k-graph tensors, or CUDA init. Data dir and pre-installed packages need verification first. Decision: Run diagnostic command to test imports, versions, data presence; will inform fixes (e.g. num_workers=0, map_location, edge_attr inclusion) before retraining and submitting eval.

Observation: Diagnostic in iter 2 segfaulted (exit 139) on import of ogb.graphproppred.mol_encoder.AtomEncoder (and torch_geometric), despite data files present; previous pipeline used GCN without edge_attr. Analysis: Likely extension/version mismatch in preinstalled PyG/ogb causing C++ crash on import; implementing AtomEncoder/BondEncoder locally avoids ogb entirely. Adding edge_attr to Datasets/Data objects and switching to GINEConv (standard for mol graphs) will use all provided features for stronger baseline while keeping param count low. Set num_workers=0 to avoid multiprocessing segfaults; remove excessive stdout prints, keep metrics.jsonl logging. Decision: Rewrite model/train/predict with local encoders + GINEConv, train 3 epochs, submit_eval immediately for baseline avg_precision and to measure iteration time before longer runs.

trajectory.description: Observation: Training failed with RDKit RuntimeError 'bond already exists' during MolDataset init for train (after loading data, during FP computation on ~350k mols). No eval score yet after 5 iters; PyG imports segfaulted previously. Analysis: OGB mol graphs are undirected so edge_index lists both (u,v) and (v,u) for each bond, causing duplicate AddBond calls on RWMol. FP computation also slow without caching (will dominate first iter). Decision: Fix get_morgan_fingerprint to add bonds only if start < end, add optional caching of fps tensors to disk (train_fps.pt) for fast future iters, keep full data + 3 epochs, rerun train then submit_eval for first avg_precision baseline and time measurement.

trajectory.description: Observation: Training crashed with RDKit RuntimeError 'RingInfo not initialized' in GetMorganFingerprintAsBitVect (after loading data and starting FP computation on train set). No eval score after 6 iters; PyG segfaults previously, RDKit bond duplicate fixed last iter but now ring init issue. Val data loaded but unused, no metric computation. Analysis: try/except pass on SanitizeMol hides failures for some reconstructed mols from OGB graphs (missing ring info for Morgan FP); FP compute slow without full cache success. Adding UpdatePropertyCache(strict=False) + GetSymmSSSR(mol) initializes rings reliably. Adding val_dataset with cache, post-train AP computation (per-task with NaN mask via sklearn, mean AP), and logging to metrics.jsonl gives primary metric visibility. Predict.py needs matching FP fix. Decision: Update both files with robust FP func + imports, enhance train.py with val eval for avg_precision baseline, run 2 epochs (time-aware), submit_eval for first score and iter timing.

trajectory.description: Observation: Working FP+MLP baseline achieved avg_precision=0.1633 (val AP 0.1712) after fixing RDKit mol reconstruction and caching fps; training used only 2 epochs with last loss ~0.044, caches now exist for fast future runs. No PyG path due to prior segfaults on imports. Analysis: Extreme imbalance addressed poorly so far (no pos_weight); short training + no scheduler + last-model-save limits performance. Per-epoch val AP (per-task, NaN-masked via sklearn) + best checkpoint + AdamW + cosine LR + pos_weights (computed from train labels) + 10 epochs (now feasible post-cache) will improve without arch changes or new dependencies. Incremental edit to train.py only (model/predict unchanged for compatibility). Decision: Rewrite train.py with these enhancements, best.pt saving, metrics logging for val_ap; run training then submit_eval on best checkpoint for updated primary metric.

Proper per-epoch metric logging will enable curve tracking. Since time allows (~1min/epoch), increase to 25 epochs for further improvement without changing architecture (avoids load issues). Fix any loss masking to average only over labeled entries. Decision: Update train.py with complete code including proper masked BCE loss, val AP computation using sklearn per-task, ReduceLROnPlateau scheduler, metrics.jsonl logging each epoch, best model saving based on val AP. trajectory.description: Observation: Training crashed with ValueError: Input contains NaN in sklearn average_precision_score (on y_score/preds) after pos weights and data load; previous best val AP ~0.1708. No jsonl metrics so curves uninformative. History shows unmasked NaNs in labels for BCE loss (causing NaN gradients/weights/outputs), incomplete valid=~np.i in val func, and scheduler/verbose issues before. Analysis: Root cause is lack of is_labeled masking in loss (NaNs propagate), no nan_to_num/try-except in AP per-task, no grad clipping (exploding grads with pos_weight+imbalance). FP+MLP baseline is stable but can be improved with BN for stability, lower dropout, more epochs, proper logging. PyG still avoided due to past segfaults. Decision: Update model.py (add BN1d + dropout=0.1 for stability/conv), fully rewrite train.py (masked loss, robust per-task AP with nan handling, grad clip, ReduceLROnPlateau, per-epoch jsonl logging, 20 epochs, best.pt on val_ap), keep predict.py; run training then submit_eval on best.pt for fixed pipeline + improved avg_precision.

trajectory.description: Observation: Training produced NaN loss and very low val AP of 0.024 due to unmasked NaN labels propagating to loss and likely NaN weights in best.pt, causing eval to fail with no valid tasks (predictions contain nulls from NaNs). Previous good runs hit ~0.163 AP. Analysis: Root cause is BCE loss on NaN targets (no is_labeled mask), incomplete sklearn AP handling for all-nan/constant tasks, no grad clip; BN in model may exacerbate instability. FP caching works but pipeline broke on masking. Decision: Rewrite model.py to simple stable MLP (no BN), fully update train.py with masked loss (nan_to_num + is_labeled mean), robust per-task AP (filter positives), per-epoch jsonl logging, ReduceLROnPlateau, grad clip, 15 epochs; minor predict.py update for nan_to_num(0.5) safety. Train then submit_eval on best.pt to recover good AP and fix eval.

Time per run ~20min safe for remaining 1.6h/2 iters. Decision: Update model.py defaults to best arch, fully rewrite complete non-truncated train.py (with all fixes/logging/seed/DataLoader) and predict.py (match arch), run training, submit_eval on best.pt to exceed 0.2191.

trajectory.description: Observation: iter29 train produced all-zero loss/val AP=0 due to truncated train.py (incomplete at 'def com', no full loop/loss/scheduler), overwriting best.pt with broken model; eval failed on state_dict mismatch (layers.20 [2048,2048] in ckpt vs [128,2048] in 6-layer model from prior 7-layer overwrites). Best remains 0.2191 (val~0.233) from iter27 with FP(r2+r3)+desc+2048/6-layer/0.1. No jsonl in recent runs (outdated curves), PyG still segfaults. Analysis: Truncation + arch drift root cause of collapse; masked BCE, DataLoader, per-epoch logging, proper nan handling (torch.where), ReduceLROnPlateau on val_ap, grad_clip are proven stable from earlier successes. Decision: Fully rewrite complete non-truncated train.py (match exact best arch=4106/2048/6/0.1, robust masked loss, sklearn AP with filters, jsonl with {'step':epoch,...}, AdamW+patience=25, 150 epochs, best.pt save); keep model.py/predict.py (consistent); run train (uses caches, ~20min), then submit_eval on new best.pt to recover+improve avg_precision before budget ends.
